pi_items.source: Task description specifies creating 'Regional Water Scarcity v1.0' method; standard LCIA water scarcity methods exist (e.g., AWARE)

pi_items.verified_value: Water Scarcity Potential (agent-created category with unit m3 eq)
pi_items.source: Water scarcity impact categories with 'm3 world eq' units are standard in LCA (AWARE method); openLCA documentation confirms this category structure
Path specified in task.json and verified in export_result.sh script (TARGET_FILE variable)
Task description requires 'at least 3 water-related flows' with characterization factors; export_result.sh queries for FACTORS_COUNT >= 3
Path specified in task.json metadata and setup_task.sh script; USLCI database is maintained by NREL and available in JSON-LD zipped format
Import USLCI task.json specifies 'import the USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory) database from the JSON-LD zip file'; USLCI official documentation confirms JSON-LD is standard format since 2003

Federal LCA Commons repository contains water-related elementary flows (freshwater, groundwater, saline water); USLCI uses Federal Elementary Flow List (FEDEFL) which includes water resource categories; verified via lcacommons.gov water flow listings

pi_items.verified_value: m3 world eq (cubic meters world equivalent)
pi_items.source: AWARE method (WULCA consensus model) standard for water scarcity assessment uses 'm3 world eq' units; openLCA implements this standard; confirmed via multiple LCA methodology sources
Setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts reference 'openLCA-data-1.4/databases'; openLCA 1.4 uses Derby database stored in this directory structure

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the USLCI (US Life Cycle Inventory) database, a real, publicly available database maintained by NREL containing over 550 process LCIs. The USLCI database is distributed in JSON-LD format as zipped files and uses the Federal Elementary Flow List (FEDEFL) which includes water resource flows (freshwater, groundwater). Water scarcity impact assessment in LCA is well-established using the AWARE (Available WAter REmaining) method with standard units of 'm3 world eq'. Water-related flows and water-consuming processes (e.g., mining, agriculture) are documented in USLCI. The task requires the agent to create a custom LCIA method and category, find at least 3 water-related flows from USLCI, assign characterization factors, and export results to a CSV file.
